sentences.text: Kate, how's it going?
What's up, Justin?
How are you?
Not too bad.
… Pathfinder, they are an ownership group.
with Sunrise and Cloud 10.
Okay, that's right.
Yeah.
And they want to bring on their San Diego, portfolio?
Sweet.
Well, Katie's kind of in charge of that.
I had met with her colleague, like, a week ago, just to.
bet.
give me the rundown on, like, what they were, like, wanting, and then he was like, let me just connect you with Katie, because she's the one that actually, … Is gonna be in charge of this, so….
Yeah, makes sense.
Cool.
Typical demo.
Sweet.
Also, side note, dude.
I signed this deal earlier this month, and then we turned off the trial to build the account, and the dude was, like, freaking out.
He's like, I signed, and I don't have access.
I was like, bro, we're… like, you had one property in there, we're building….
Yeah.
Oil.
Give us a little bit.
People were nuts, man, that's so stupid.
I'm like, did you take months to reply at a time, and then, you know, you don't have access for a couple days because we're building everything?
I'm like….
That's really funny.
Alright, I'll let her in.
Cool.
Afternoon, Katie.
How are you?
Hey, good, how are you?
I'm doing good.
Nice to meet you.
Nice to meet you.
How's your week going?
Good.
Busy week, for sure.
How about you?
Is he in a good way or a bad way?
Good, good.
Yeah, lots of just trainings, meetings, Padre games, all that good stuff.
Little bit of work, little bit of pleasure.
Yeah.
Nice.
Well, yeah, my week, my week's going pretty good, busy as well, but, busy is good for sales, so I'll take it.
Nice.
Glad that we could connect and, have this conversation, though.

I know I'd met with your, colleague, Matt, like, maybe a week or two ago, and he kind of gave… you kind of gave me the rundown on everything, but I think the conversation today is targeted around your San Diego properties, correct?
Yeah, and … I didn't get a chance, but I don't know if we're currently using your software, maybe from, like, a higher level, but … At least from my level and below, we're not using it, so….
Yeah, and I think you guys, work directly with Sunrise, like Cloud 10, right?
Yeah, I work for Sunrise.
Okay, yeah.
Well, I think, we work with, a handful of Sunrise properties.
I don't know off the top of my head which ones, but we are kind of partnered already, with you guys.
Cool, okay.
I'm curious, from, like, a reporting standpoint, have you seen any of the ApartmentIQ reports?
No.
Okay, so this'll all be brand new to you.
Yeah.
Okay.
And then, I guess let me ask you this, as far as, like, rent comp, analysis goes right now for San Diego, what's, what's the process there?
Is it manual?
So for our site teams, yes, they are manually, doing the comp surveys.
From the regional level, we have CoStar, and then… I think our business development is the one using, Apartment IQ, but I'm not sure.
Okay.
And, … You know, 3 different data sources.
I'm assuming you're kind of cross-referencing each one for decision making?

Yeah, I mean, of course, not… I'm not using ApartmentIQ for any kind of, … data analysis at this point, so yeah, I use CoStar, and CoStar is good, it doesn't have… I don't feel like it completely has current data, and so that's why the managers' market surveys do provide a little bit more live data, I think, than CoStar does.
Okay.
And do you know off the top of your head the, the frequency that your managers are kind of doing those market surveys?
Once a month.
Okay.
And then, when it comes to pricing, is that how you are pricing your units and floor plans?
Yes, essentially.
Okay.
Okay.
And then….
I mean, obviously, if we're having, like, a need at a property or something, we… We'll do it more frequently, but….
Okay.
And then, do you all use any sort of, like, … any sort of, like, internal kind of database or warehouse where you store all of your, kind of, like, market data at all, or no?
The market surveys, I mean, it's just like Excel.
Okay.
But they come to us, and we store at their teams, and….
In our share file and all that, but….
Yeah, that makes sense.
Okay.
And then last thing for you is just kind of how, how familiar are you with ApartmentIQ?
That way, we can kind of know where to fill in the gaps today.
I'm not super familiar.
I mean, I have the general idea that it's for market surveys, and kind of pulling comp data as far as, like.
It's kind of similar to CoStar, like, … rents, expenses, all of that good stuff.
I just don't know what kind of market insights it has.
Sure, yeah, gladly to, go over pretty much everything we can do.
And I guess, I don't know where it pulls the data, but that would be….
So, all of the data is 100% publicly available information.
It's all coming directly from the online leasing section of the property websites.
So, essentially, all a property has to have is an online presence for us to give you all the rent comp data, from the property level, floor plan, all the way down to the unit.
So as long as they have online leasing, we can track whoever you'd like.
And, we used to just be a market survey tool, so to your point, we can automate that entire process for you.
We give you a live data feed into your owned or managed assets, each specific comp you're tracking.
This allows you to log in and see from, like, a floor plan and unit, specific position, how are your assets compared to the competition.

So, the nice thing about that is we've been around for the last 5 years, we've been tracking all this unit-level data over the last 5 years, so we give you the ability to aggregate the data back over any given time frame that you'd like, depending on who's taking a look at the data, what sort of picture you're trying to paint for, like, seasonality and trends.
So that's kind of one functionality, is gonna be, like, the market survey.
Second is great for acquisitions or new development.
You can spin up these custom market surveys for any property in our database that you're looking to acquire, or maybe a plot of land you're looking to build on.
Takes about 3 seconds, but that's great for new development acquisitions.
Last functionality is called Explore.
It's very similar to CoStar.
It's gonna be all market, sub-market level information.

You can drill that down to the, neighborhood, you can create custom, radiuses, and the nice thing about that is, it's visual, but then also has graphs and overlays, which I'm a big fan of, and … in that, you'll get the 5 years of historicals, you'll get demographic information, and then we break out in stages all of the new supply and construction pipeline for you as well.
So, used to just be a market survey tool.
All the conversations that Justin and I have on these calls, it seems like all these companies are using so many different data providers, and they don't love all of them.
There's bits and pieces in each one that they love, or a manual process, so we got the feedback, where could you just marry all these data sources together in one?
And that's kind of what we built out.
Nice.
So, we'll share our screen, and then we'll walk you through it.
Cool.
Cool.
Yeah, Katie, I'll give you the high level of it here, but it's a pretty simple interface.
like Hunter said, 3 different parts of the tool.
The first one, the My Property section, this would be your portfolio's market surveys.
The way this is laid out is that each one of your properties would get their own individual property card, and then all the data is based off of that property-specific comp set.
Just to let you know, your team's in control of your comp set, so typically what happens whenever we implement a customer is that they'll provide us with the list of comps they've been tracking.
We'll start there.

However, we have a comp matching algorithm, so we can make suggestions or recommendations for you, but what's nice is, in the actual app, you can always add or remove a property to your comps at any time you'd like.
So, total control of it.
But you click into a… into a property card.
Once you're in here, the very top will be your main navigation.
So each property in here has 4 main reports associated with it.
Just a reminder that once you are in here, the data is updated as of this morning.
But you do have the ability to look at the data over whatever timeframe you are most interested in.
So, if you're having a client call or maybe an investor call, just know that you can do a monthly or quarterly review if you'd like.
So, whatever's relevant to the conversation.
We do have both PDF and Excel exports that can be downloaded anytime you'd like.
They can also be automated to be delivered to an inbox on whatever frequency or cadence you'd like as well.
And then in the top right, this is where you would actually go to edit your comps, so if you ever want to add or remove… move a property, you just come to this section.
On the right, we give you a map view of the area.
So the blue pin would be your property, the white pins or property is already added to your comp set, and then the black pins or properties are in the area you might want to consider adding as well.
So, it's as simple as clicking Add Comp, all the data plus historicals are added.
Yeah.
Fuck.
Major call-out over here on the left with this list view is just gonna be this comp score.

So, our system assigns a score to every property in the area of yours, but it's a correlation score, or essentially a reference point in terms of how strong our system thinks a comp is compared to your property.
So, up to you, you can track as few or as many as you'd like.
But inside of here, the market survey, I mean, it should be pretty familiar to you.
It's gonna be broken out into 5 different sections.
The first one being just property summary, you know, how far is each comp from you, who manages it?
We give you links out to the, the Google reviews.
We do let you know if there are rent specials or concessions tied to the property, or even specific floor plans or units on the property.
And then, as you can see, we do display, both gross and net effective rents.
Down below that, you'll see that we show your performance metrics, so your occupancy, leased exposure percentages.
We can hit on availability, and then we do show things like applications, cancels, denials, and leases.
Sorry, cut off for a second there.
How does it pull, like, advertised occupancy?
Is that just, like, does it kind of grab how many units they're reflecting as open, or…?

Yeah, so the way that… and the reason we call this calculated metrics is because… just because it's publicly available data, so if you have some downed units, for example, we don't necessarily know that.
But for all of these metrics down here, it has to do with the fact that we're tracking unit-level data every single day.
So for occupancy, we can just see the number of vacant units in a property, and then do the math based off the total units, and figure it out from there.
I'll let you know in regards to, like, apps, cancels, and leases.
The way this works is basically if a unit's available on the property website today, it follows under the available units bucket.

If tomorrow that unit's gone from the property website, we'll assume that somebody put in an application on the unit, and then if that unit doesn't reappear on the property website within 7 days, meaning it wasn't canceled, then it becomes a lease.
That's the science behind how you report these performance metrics with publicly available data, but always spot on.
I'll say the only discrepancy you might see if you would audit your own numbers is that internally, you guys might process an application in 3 days or 4 days, whereas we have a 7-day grace period.
So….
Makes sense.
Yeah.
We will show pricing and availability here, broken up by floor plan.
So this is just, like, a very quick and dirty, you know, where are my studios, where are my one-bedrooms positioned compared to the comps.
What's nice is inside of here, we do give you every performance metric you might care about.
So perhaps you're basing performance off of rent or price per foot, just easily being able to sort this from high to low, low to high, and seeing where you stack up.
Oh.
Fees and deposits are included, so helping you be aware of the ancillary fees or revenue opportunities out there.
And then last but not least, will be the in-unit or community-level amenities as well.
Is that… do you know if that's kind of what it uses to create that score?
It's part of it, yeah.
So, in regards to the score, we look at 5 factors, one being how far the property is from your property, how far the comp is.
We also look at vintage, total units, and then we do pricing and amenity matching.
So those 5 factors go into what spits out the score.

So, I always say, like, these scores should be, like, objectively correct, but at the same time, I mean, if you know your markets, I mean, you know, it can be a little bit subjective, but it's up to you.
Yeah, or I mean, like, for example, we have a property that they're… they… Pathfinder likes to purchase, like, value-add deals, so….
Yep.
I think our comp set is not what the… condition of the current property is, so, I mean.
For sure.
Customize it.
Yeah, and along those same lines there, what's nice, too, is if Pathfinder comes to you and they say, hey, you know, we're looking at acquiring a property, you can come to this research tool.
And what you can do is click New Report.
And you can type in the name or the address of whatever property they're looking to purchase.
And, like, let's say, for example, they're looking at the Clio, the one in Dallas here.
You find it, you click on it, and in, like, 3 seconds, you have a real-time market survey for that property.
So it can be helpful for that underwriting process.
Yeah, and I saw there was an option to do value add.
Yeah, so what that'll do, if you flip value add-on, is it'll actually bring back comps that are about 15-20% higher than what the property would currently command.
Yeah.
So, what I would say is, if you're doing, like, a value-add property, or, like, a value-add acquisition, kind of pro forma, is run it once normally.

Run it once with, like, the value add comps toggled on, and then you can kind of do the comparisons there, like, hey, this is where it sits right now, this is where it sits, … You know, from the value-add standpoint.
And then what you can do is you can actually, like, rename these things whatever you want.
So, like, if you call it the Clio, you can say, you know, this is, like, this is the Clio… you know, renovated comp set, or whatever, you know, you can customize it.
Okay.
I think what's nice too, actually, is if you are analyzing the market, we have the filtering options where you can actually include or exclude renovated units and classic units as well.
Lots of filtering options for you.

Yeah, and even from a floor plan perspective, so if we lay this out for you, and we can show you the averages based off, you know, 1s, twos, and 3s, but what's cool is if you toggle over, we can break down and give you each individual floor plan within a property as well.
Hmm.
So, the idea with this is, like, you know, if we look at the 1x1s, that not all one-bedrooms compete with all other one-bedrooms.
So, we can see the smallest one-bed in this comp set starts at 516 feet at the alcove.
If I scroll all the way to the bottom, the largest one's gonna be, you know, closer to 1,000 square feet here at the Harlow.
So our goal with this report is to make it very easy to compare your floor plans off of like or similar sized floor plans.
So, probably more so from, like, the operations side, or making sure you're priced appropriately.
Bye.
You follow the data, and we'll give you unit mix at the floor plan level, as well as leased availability and rent trend data over whatever time frame you are interested in.
And you can toggle between gross and net effective runs.
Oh, cool.
Yeah.

Hunter mentioned it, but we do expose all 5 years' worth of our historical data in the application, so it seems like post-pandemic, we're back to some normalization in terms of trends and seasonality, so it can be helpful there, and I think, you know, what's nice here is you can pick and choose your timeframe, you can select your interval, and then you can look at the data at the property level where broken up by floor plan.
But inside of here, we'll give you a rent trends, so both gross and net.
Like, right now, the way that we're displaying this is we're comparing our property's performance versus the median of the comp set.
But if you want, you can toggle over and look at each individual's property's performance over that given time frame as well.
Cool.
different presentations there, but we'll show you rent trends, we'll give you concession history, so you can look at concession history as a percentage of rent or as a value.
We'll give you supply.
leasing velocity.
And also your occupancy, lease, and exposure percentage trends.

So, lots of data here, and again, I think the biggest thing is that it's gonna be automated, the data's gonna be updated daily, and I always say, like, the… I think the best part about You know, sourcing publicly available information, is that if your team ever has a question about where a data point, a metric, a calculation, a concession, you know, occupancy, how… anything in here, we can trace it all back to the source.
So, it's not like we talked to a, you know, a leasing agent, they said, oh, you're at 98%, but we've got a 2-month, you know, free concession on the website.
So, easy to, easy to understand it.
Buh.

And then, yeah, Explore, you also have access to Explore, so like Hunter said, this is, this is CoStar-esque, but you can type in any city, town, neighborhood, zip code, county, you can draw your own boundary, but inside of here, you'll get rent comp data.
So if I drill into this really quickly for San Diego, you'll get, … Give it a second.
Maybe.
So you'll get a 30-day snapshot, which includes performance metrics, as well as averages broken out by bed type.
you'll get those same historical data points I just talked to you about a second ago as well.
So your rent trends, your supply by bed type.
Your leasing velocity by bed type, and also those occupancy, leasing exposure percentage trends.
If you look at the map there, you'll notice 4 different colored pins on the map.
So the black pins, they represent properties that exist.
And then the blue, pink, and yellow pins actually represent properties under different stages of construction.
And if you put your mouse over the pins, we can give you details on the construction projects.
As well as over here on the left, we can show you how many units and properties make up each stage of construction, as well as when they're slated to be delivered by quarter.
So just being aware of the new supply that's coming on board.
Unfortunately, I don't think it's all going to hit during peak leasing season, so obviously that influx of supply can change things.
Yeah.
And then demographics, so population, education, income, employment data.

And what's nice about this is rather than just giving it to you at the market or sub-market level, you can actually take it down to specific neighborhoods or zip codes, or even based off a custom-defined boundary.
So, obviously, the more granularity can tell a much different story than just a general market or submarket.
Yeah, nice.
Yeah, I mean, it's nice, you can find.
Does it give you, like… and sorry, you might have said this, does it give you, like, a highlight as to when, deliveries are expected and, like, how many units?
It does, yeah.
Yeah, so over here on the left… let me scroll back out.
Let me get out of Pacific Beach.
Nothing in Pacific Beach being built right now.
Yeah, so if you, … Where are we?
Yeah, right here for new supply.
So it'll show you how many units and properties make up each stage.
And then over here, or down below here, it shows you when they're gonna be delivered by quarter.
Of course.
Looks like we've got a few being delivered here, we've got about 1,000 units being delivered in, at the end of this year.
And then next year will be… looks like it's gonna be pretty big.
Interesting.
Okay, I mean, that's really cool to see it from that level, because I don't think we see that on CoStar.
For sure.
Yeah, like I said, I mean, I typed in Pacific Beach, you can type… you should be able to type in, like, Gaslamp District, and… Er, maybe not.
… Yeah, you can type in, like, Gaslamp.
You can get as granular as you want with these, … these reports here.

You can even type in property addresses or, like, points of interest as well, so if you want to see this data based off a certain radius around a property, or even if you're like, hey, I want this data from, you know, San Diego State, you know, give me all this data from 3 miles from San Diego State, you can get all the information that way.
But, yeah, I think, like Hunter said, I mean, it's gonna be the best rent comp data you can get in the industry.
We're very confident about that.
It's just now kind of giving you the supply, the demographics, some of the other data points that are available out there.
So….
But yeah, I mean, for what it's worth, too, from the market survey side, we can set up anybody that's relevant to a property.
So, site teams, operations, revenue, marketing, you know, anybody who has an association with the property, we can set them up.
We also offer, full training, so group sessions, one-on-one sessions, ongoing training sessions as you get new employees.
So we do a nice job of making sure whoever's relevant to the property feels comfortable navigating the platform.
And then finally, as far as setup goes, it's the lightest lift possible.
There's zero integrations.
It's literally just giving us a list of your properties, any preferred comps, and we set these things up in, like, a business week.
Oh, cool, okay.
Yep.
Yeah, and I guess… I guess I would… I can find out, maybe today, or whatever, if… I don't know if you guys have the info, if we are using it, in fact, at the corporate level with Sunrise, ….
And how that works as far as, like.
Is, do we have one account for corporate, and then we set up each property with a different account, or…?
How you guys work on that?
Aspect.
Give me a second.
I can tell you right now… There's about 30 properties on right now.
Oh, a sunrise?
Yeah, so technically, like, in our CRM, I wasn't a part of this one.
It's under Cloud 10.
Hmm, okay.
Which is, like, maybe you could explain that a little bit better, right?
Is it, like.
Yeah, so Sandy, or Sunrise, …

was based, like, started in San Diego, and we've branched out to different markets, so we're in, like, Pacific Northwest, Nevada, Arizona, but a year or two ago, we split off, so California is Sunrise, and we made those properties Cloud 10, so… We're still sister companies, we still interact and use the same, like, HR and stuff, but it's two separate companies.
Justin, so I'm assuming then in that position, we would probably just make their own, like, Sunrise instance, right?
Because that one's probably living underneath Cloud 10.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, you'd have your own Sunrise landing page and everything.
Yeah, it'd be like your… We kind of treat them as sister companies, too.
I mean, yeah, you'd have your own dashboard and everything.

Okay, yeah, I'm just curious, because I… I mean, I am… A fan, and of course, will be a proponent of rolling it out, but… Just not sure if it's more of a, like, a one-account corporate type thing, or if… How we would set each property up if that's structured differently within your guys' company, but….
Yeah, I mean, we can do whatever makes the most sense for you guys.
So….
Okay.
Do you… well, I guess, do we pay per user?
It's a… it's a per unit, per month cost.
So, … We could, I mean, we could just match the rate of, like, the Cloud 10.
Okay.
Okay.
Yeah, I guess maybe some logistics to look into, because the client specifically has asked me to look into this.
And so, I'm looking into it for his properties.
not, like, our company.
So, anyway, I think maybe it's just a conversation with my supervisor, who's the VP of SoCal, and just seeing where they're at with it between us and Cloud 10, and see how we can maybe
Meet the needs of the client.
Yeah, totally.
Roll it out, you know, if they don't want to roll it out company-wide.
Perfect.
Oh, bro.
… More than… more than happy as well to, kind of reconnect after that and talk through it, where… If it's just San Diego, more than happy to do that.
If it's more of, like, a broader scale, … can obviously work with you on, like, price for that as well, for… if it's more units than San Diego.
So, we're flexible, just here to be resources for you, so… or if all of us need to hop on a call and talk through it, more than happy to do that.
That is what we're here for.
Cool, sounds good.
Awesome.
Well, thanks, Katie.
Yeah.
Let me send you over a, kind of like a quick slide deck that just covers everything we went over.
Circle it internally, have the conversations you need to have, and then, I just say keep the line of communication open.
Let us know, kind of, what's the best next steps for us to work through or talk through.
Okay, sounds good.
Yeah, I will connect with the client, and then also, Daniel and who's with Sunrise, and get back to you.
Awesome.
Sounds good.
Thanks, Katie.
Alrighty, thanks, have a good one.
